page: Microsoft Azure D192ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 96 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores with hyper-threading enabled. Operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz, the processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. The server is equipped with 768 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4 GB of RAM per vCPU. Additionally, it bundles 11339 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple compute and memory metrics. The multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and the PassMark CPU score both rank in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, with read-only small block cached speeds and large block uncached write speeds placing in the top 10%, though the overall PassMark memory score falls in the average range. The system excels in data processing, achieving top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, Redis SET operations, and database operations. For machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference shows strong prompt processing capabilities across 135M, 7B, and 70B models, though text generation speeds vary from weak for small models to average for large models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D192ds_v7 offers high resource density by packing 192 vCPUs and over 11 TB of local storage into a single instance, reducing the need for multi-node cluster management for large-scale workloads. However, the lack of dedicated GPU hardware represents a clear tradeoff, limiting its efficiency for heavy parallel deep learning training despite its strong CPU-based matrix math capabilities. This hardware profile is highly suited for large relational databases, high-throughput web serving, intensive data compression pipelines, and enterprise-grade general-purpose applications that require massive parallel processing and substantial local storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D192ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv7 family, powered by 192 dedicated vCPUs on 96 physical Intel Xeon 6973P-C cores running at 4.2 GHz. It features 768 GB of RAM, 11339 GB of local storage, and supports Premium Storage. The server delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and exceptional database operation speeds, ranking in the top 10% in multiple benchmarks. While it lacks dedicated GPU accelerators, its support for Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions and AVX-512 enables strong CPU-based prompt processing for large language models. This configuration offers high resource density for enterprise workloads, making it highly suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and intensive data processing pipelines.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D192ds_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB of RAM, and 11339 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier database and multi-core compute performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D192ds_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and 11339 GB local storage for high-throughput enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density virtual machine combining massive multi-core Intel Xeon compute with extensive local storage for demanding enterprise database and data processing workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6973P-C processors at 4.2 GHz
768 GB of system memory and 11339 GB of local storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated CPU-based vector and matrix operations
Best suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and data compression
